# STATUS DEFINITIONS:
# ==================
# Epic Status:
#   - backlog: Epic not yet started
#   - in-progress: Epic actively being worked on
#   - done: All stories in epic completed
#
# Epic Status Transitions:
#   - backlog → in-progress: Automatically when first story is created (via create-story)
#   - in-progress → done: Manually when all stories reach 'done' status
#
# Story Status:
#   - backlog: Story only exists in epic file
#   - ready-for-dev: Story file created in stories folder
#   - in-progress: Developer actively working on implementation
#   - review: Ready for code review (via Dev's code-review workflow)
#   - done: Story completed
#
# Retrospective Status:
#   - optional: Can be completed but not required
#   - done: Retrospective has been completed
#
# WORKFLOW NOTES:
# ===============
# - Epic transitions to 'in-progress' automatically when first story is created
# - Stories can be worked in parallel if team capacity allows
# - Developer typically creates next story after previous one is 'done' to incorporate learnings
# - Dev moves story to 'review', then runs code-review (fresh context, different LLM recommended)

**✅ Code review actions complete**

- Decision-needed resolved: **2** (both → Patch)
- Patches handled: **4** (all applied in working tree — not yet committed)
- Deferred: **1** (appended to `deferred-work.md`)
- Dismissed: **11**

---

**Review Complete!**

- **Story Status:** `done`
- **Issues Fixed:** 4
- **Action Items Created:** 0
- **Deferred:** 1
- **Dismissed:** 11

Changes in working tree (not committed — the original commit `84affc3` is unchanged; these patches are a follow-up):
- `CONTRIBUTING.md` — 2 bullets rewritten (Epic 6 jargon removed, quote truncation fixed, double-negative tightened; prettier absorbed one formatting pass)
- `package.json` — all five `release*` script echoes now redirect to stderr (`>&2 && exit 1 ;`)

Sprint-status.yaml synced (Story 4.2 → `done`).

**Heads-up on the stderr divergence:** AC 5 of the story pins the prefix string verbatim. Adding `>&2` is a deliberate spec divergence per your Decision 1 call — I noted this inline in the story's Review Findings so Story 5.1 readiness audit and Story 6.2 delete-script pass will see the reason.

---

**What would you like to do next?**

1. **Start the next story** — run `dev-story` to pick up the next `ready-for-dev` story (Epic 5 is backlog; Story 5.1 would be the next to create via `bmad-create-story`)
2. **Re-run code review** — address findings and review again
3. **Done** — end the workflow

**HALT** — reply `1`, `2`, or `3`.

input.command:
git add CONTRIBUTING.md package.json && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
chore(release): apply code-review patches (Story 4.2)

Four patches from the Story 4.2 code review on commit 84affc3:

- package.json: all five `release*` script echoes now redirect to
  stderr via `>&2`, following the convention for diagnostic /
  deprecation notices. Deliberate divergence from Story 4.2 AC 5's
  verbatim prefix string — flagged in the story's Review Findings
  so Story 5.1 readiness audit and Story 6.2 delete pass see the
  intent. Fail-loud contract preserved: all five keys exit 1 with
  `[DEPRECATED]` on stderr, no tag mutations, no version bump
  (re-verified via the same smoke loop used in Task 4).
- CONTRIBUTING.md `## Releasing` / npm-run bullet: truncated
  `[DEPRECATED] Use ... GitHub Actions.` quote replaced with a
  shorter "a `[DEPRECATED]` warning pointing at the canonical
  path" form — the prior quote misrepresented the real echo
  message. Double-negative "muscle memory doesn't accidentally
  ship anything" tightened to positive "catch muscle-memory
  `npm run release` invocations before they ship anything".
- CONTRIBUTING.md both `## Releasing` bullets: internal "Epic 6"
  label replaced with "a post-v1.0.0 cleanup pass". External
  contributors reading CONTRIBUTING.md get the when/why without
  needing to resolve an internal epics.md reference.
- CONTRIBUTING.md unrelated prettier idempotence fix absorbed
  (`*why*` → `_why_` in `## Workflow for Changes`). Prettier would
  rewrite this on the next edit anyway; folding it in here keeps
  the working tree clean.

Verification: `node -e 'JSON.parse(...)'` OK; `prettier --check`
OK; `markdownlint-cli2 CONTRIBUTING.md` 0 errors; 5-script
fail-loud smoke loop PASS.

Review also dismissed 11 findings (noise or verified safe) and
deferred 1 (CONTRIBUTING.md → docs/RELEASING.md anchor not
CI-guarded; captured in _bmad-output/.../deferred-work.md as a
pre-existing validator-coverage gap).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
